fact: December 7 is exactly 18 days before Christmas, which means reindeer are probably doing their final flight training right about now!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun sets so early on December 7 that some places get dark before 4:30 PM - that's earlier than most kids finish their after-school snacks!
December 7 falls during Sagittarius season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposedly extra adventurous and might randomly decide to climb a tree or explore their basement.
Polar bears in the Arctic are typically building their snow dens around this time, creating cozy ice caves that can be 40 degrees warmer inside than outside!
December 7 is only 14 days away from the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere - the shortest day of the year when some places get less than 6 hours of sunlight!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, December 7 is prime summer prep time when cicadas start making their loud buzzing sounds that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
December 7 is the 341st day of the year, leaving exactly 24 days left - which means there are only 576 hours remaining until New Year's Eve!

Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are often at their most fragrant around December 7 in northern climates because the cold weather makes their sap thicker and more concentrated with that fresh Christmas tree smell.

In northern regions, December 7 typically marks the time when lakes and ponds start forming their first serious ice layers - thick enough for small animals to walk on but definitely not thick enough for humans!
When there's a full moon around December 7, the extra light reflects off snow and ice, making winter nights in snowy regions almost as bright as dawn!
December 7 sits perfectly in the season when your breath becomes visible in cold air - this happens when the temperature drops below about 45°F and your warm exhaled air hits the chilly atmosphere!
